description_long: This object is a classic 3.5-inch floppy disk, commonly used for data storage in the late 20th century. The disk features a square, flat, black plastic shell with a central metallic shutter that protects the magnetic disk inside. On the front, there is a white label with a purple header, marked 'KYANOS INDEX' and handwritten text reading 'Pictures #2', indicating its use for organizing digital files. The disk has a small rectangular cutout in the lower right corner, which serves as a write-protect switch. The back is mostly featureless except for a faint circular indentation and some molded details. Its compact and lightweight design made it a portable medium for transferring files between computers.
description: A black 3.5-inch floppy disk with a white label and purple header, featuring handwritten text and a metallic shutter.
description_view_0: Front view showing the label with 'KYANOS INDEX' and handwritten 'Pictures #2', as well as the write-protect notch.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the thinness of the disk and the edge of the label.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the plain black plastic back with a faint circular indentation.
description_view_3: Opposite angled side view, again emphasizing the disk's slim profile and the edge of the metallic shutter.
